Additional information: name: Save Your Wardrobe description: Plateforme numérique de garde-robe qui recueille les achats passés de mode et rationalise l'expérience post-achat grâce à un écosystème de services après-vente. industry: Fashiontech country: Tunisia city: Tunis website: entity_type: startup website_content: []( * About * Products * Resources * News Contact Us Contact Us # We are the global standard for end-to-end aftersales solutions, powering every service beyond the point of sale. Our platform streamlines the entire post-purchase ecosystem—covering repairs, care, alterations, warranties, logistics, exchanges, compliance, customer experience, and data reporting. We give brands the infrastructure to operate efficiently, deliver exceptional customer experiences, and unlock long-term value from every product they sell. Contact Us We empower brands to map, design, automate, and scale aftersales processes—delivering the service infrastructure modern retailers need to operate intelligently and transform every post-purchase interaction into measurable value. Save Your Wardrobe is trusted by # The Aftersales Platform Trusted by the World’s Leading Brands Save Your Wardrobe’s full product suite is a modular, end-to-end solution that digitises and streamlines aftersales operations, enabling brands to scale repair, care, and alteration services effortlessly. Built for omnichannel integration, the platform seamlessly connects with ERP systems, warranty workflows, and loyalty programmes, while offering API-based interoperability for flexible deployment across digital and in-store touchpoints. Learn More End-to-End Aftersales Solution Designed to enhance operational efficiency, our dynamic booking engine enables service requests to be initiated and tracked in real time—whether through a brand’s website, app, or in-store interface. The Atelier Management Solution streamlines workflow automation, delivering granular oversight across repairs, logistics, and customer communications. With a robust admin dashboard and live data insights, brands gain full visibility into service performance, sustainability impact, and product lifecycle extension—driving both customer engagement and circularity outcomes. Activité BQube ITs est une start-up spécialiste en 
Industrie4.0. Elle offre la solution QMES en mode SAAS 
(Software as a Service) exploitable au sein ou en dehors 
de la structure (accessible de partout), qui permet 
une gestion et un contrôle h24 du déroulement des 
activités. Afin de permettre les entreprises industrielles 
de maîtriser tous les détails du processus de production 
qui ont un impact négatif sur l’efficacité et la qualité de 
leurs opérations QMES 4.0 a été conçu pour: -Avoir une 
meilleure gestion du flux de l'usine -Améliorer les pertes 
de performance, de qualité et de disponibilité -Faire le 
suivi de l'usine en temps réel. -Analyser le rendement et 
l'identification des problèmes. QMES 4.0 est également 
capable de prévoir les risques via un système de 
prédiction des défauts et de la production.
Aspect Technologique L'application QMES est Full Web, 
en mode SAAS, hébergée EN CLOUD ou en Serveur 
locale dépendamment du besoin du client, communique 
directement avec les automates et les capteurs (IoT) 
pour la collecte automatique des données.
Originalité Application performante et interactive: le temps 
de réponse permet de gérer un nombre considérable de 
transactions simultanées (Actuellement, la solution gère 
une production de 3 pièces par seconde et par ligne 
chez une industrie comportant 30 lignes). -Protocole de 
communication standard et multicanal. - La solution est en 
mode SAAS (Hébergé en Cloud ou sur un serveur local) 
accessible à distance permettant le contrôle et le suivi h24 
via tout appareil connecté. -La solution est développée 
d'une manière à obéir parfaitement aux attentes des 
professionnelles en industrie grâce aux collaborateurs qui 
sont des consultants métiers et qui maîtrisent les besoins. 
-La modularité: QMES intègre plusieurs modules à la fois: 
Traçabilité, qualité et production.
Activity BQube ITs is a start-up specialized in Industry 
4.0. It offers the QMES solution in SAAS mode (Software 
as a Service), which can be used inside or outside the 
company (accessible from anywhere), and which allows 
a -24hour management and control of the activities. In 
order to allow industrial companies to control all the 
details of the production process that have a negative 
impact on the efficiency and quality of their operations 
QMES 4.0 has been designed to - Have a better 
management of the plant flow - Improve performance, 
quality and availability losses -Monitor the plant in real 
time. -Analyze performance and identify problems. QMES 
4.0 is also capable of predicting risks via a defect and 
production prediction system.
Technological Aspect The QMES application is Full Web, 
in SAAS mode, hosted in the cloud or on a local server 
depending on the customer's needs, communicates 
directly with the automatons and sensors (IoT) for 
automatic data collection.
Originality Powerful and interactive application: the 
response time allows managing a considerable number 
of simultaneous transactions (Currently, the solution 
manages a production of 3 parts per second and per line 
in an industry with 30 lines). Standard and multi-channel 
communication protocol. - The solution is in SAAS mode 
(hosted in the cloud or on a local server) and is remotely 
accessible, allowing control and monitoring 24 hours a 
day via any connected device. -The solution is developed 
in such a way as to perfectly meet the expectations of 
industry professionals, thanks to the employees who 
are business consultants and who master the needs. 
-Modularity: QMES integrates several modules at once: 
Traceability, quality and production.

EN

Elle offre la solution QMES en mode SAAS (Software as a Service) exploitable au sein ou en dehors de la structure (accessible de partout), qui permet une gestion et un contrôle h24 du déroulement des activités. Afin de permettre les entreprises industrielles de maîtriser tous les détails du processus de production qui ont un impact négatif sur l’efficacité et la qualité de leurs opérations QMES 4.0 a été conçu pour: -Avoir une meilleure gestion du flux de l'usine -Améliorer les pertes de performance, de qualité et de disponibilité -Faire le suivi de l'usine en temps réel. -Analyser le rendement et l'identification des problèmes. QMES 4.0 est également capable de prévoir les risques via un système de prédiction des défauts et de la production. Aspect Technologique L'application QMES est Full Web, en mode SAAS, hébergée EN CLOUD ou en Serveur locale dépendamment du besoin du client, communique directement avec les automates et les capteurs (IoT) pour la collecte automatique des données. Originalité Application performante et interactive: le temps de réponse permet de gérer un nombre considérable de transactions simultanées (Actuellement, la solution gère une production de 3 pièces par seconde et par ligne chez une industrie comportant 30 lignes). -Protocole de communication standard et multicanal. - La solution est en mode SAAS (Hébergé en Cloud ou sur un serveur local) accessible à distance permettant le contrôle et le suivi h24 via tout appareil connecté. -La solution est développée d'une manière à obéir parfaitement aux attentes des professionnelles en industrie grâce aux collaborateurs qui sont des consultants métiers et qui maîtrisent les besoins. -La modularité: QMES intègre plusieurs modules à la fois: Traçabilité, qualité et production. Activity BQube ITs is a start-up specialized in Industry 4.0. It offers the QMES solution in SAAS mode (Software as a Service), which can be used inside or outside the company (accessible from anywhere), and which allows a -24hour management and control of the activities. In order to allow industrial companies to control all the details of the production process that have a negative impact on the efficiency and quality of their operations QMES 4.0 has been designed to - Have a better management of the plant flow - Improve performance, quality and availability losses -Monitor the plant in real time. -Analyze performance and identify problems. QMES 4.0 is also capable of predicting risks via a defect and production prediction system. Technological Aspect The QMES application is Full Web, in SAAS mode, hosted in

Activité "Ferid autour du monde" est une application 
mobile ludique et éducative, qui offrira aux enfants des 
contenus pédagogiques tels que des histoires, des vidéos 
animées, des jeux, des quiz, sous forme de parcours 
interactifs. Notre application aide à engager les enfants 
en leur fournissant un contenu différent en fonction de 
leurs types d'intelligence.
Aspect 
Technologique 
Notre 
application 
mobile 
est 
disponible sur la majorité des appareils mobiles, 
notamment Android, iPhone, IPad, et sur le web.
Originalité Notre originalité est notre méthodologie de 
gamification, qui augmente l'engagement de l'utilisateur, 
sa concentration et sa volonté d'apprendre. Notre 
application récompense les progrès des utilisateurs par 
des réalisations,et des prix pour les personnages. De plus, 
"Ferid" se concentre sur la rétention des utilisateurs grâce 
à des classements dans le jeu et à la narration de contenu 
dans les jeux, les histoires et les vidéos.
Activity "Ferid around the world" is a fun and 
educational mobile application that will offer children 
educational content such as stories, animated videos, 
games, quizzes, in the format of an interactive journey. 
Our application helps to engage children by providing 
them with different content according to their types of 
intelligence.
Technological Aspect Our mobile application is available on 
most mobile devices, including Android, iPhone, IPad, and 
on the web.
Originality Our originality is our gamification methodology, 
which increases user engagement, focus and willingness 
to learn. Our application rewards user progress with 
achievements, and character prizes. In addition, "Ferid" 
focuses on user retention through in-game leaderboards 
and content storytelling in games, stories and videos.

Activité TadreeX est une solution en ligne oÙ les 
employés peuvent suivre une formation industrielle à

représente pour le département des ressources humaines 
un nouvel outil pédagogique qui offre un apprentissage 
pratique, une évaluation en temps réel des apprenants 
et un engagement accru des employés. En fonction des 
besoins des clients, notre solution peut être déployée 
sur des casques de réalité virtuelle VR (High immersive 
Technology) ou via des ordinateurs (Low Immersive 
Technology) sur notre plateforme web www. tadreex.com.
Aspect Technologique Immersive Learning : L'utilisation de 
la réalité virtuelle et l'immersion en 360 degré avec les 
casques de réalité virtuelle.

de

en

is 
an 
online 
solution 
where 
employees can take industrial training using 3D immersive

increased employee engagement. Depending on the

المِنَصَّة التَّعْلِيمية تَدْرِيس دُرُوس دَعْم وتدَارُك عن بُعْد ## فِي دَاركْ ... اِتْهَنَّىْ عَلَى قَرَايَة إِصْغَارك تَدْرِيس هي منصّة تعليميّة عبر الإنترنت في تونس موجّهة لتلامذة الابتدائي والأساسي والثّانوي، نقدّم دروس دعم في شكل فيديوهات مسجّلة لشرح كلّ دروس البرنامج الدّراسي وحصص مباشرة تفاعليّة تحت إشراف ثلّة من الأساتذة والمربّين من ذوي الخبرة من كامل أنحاء الجمهورية. **اِشْتَرِكْ وَ إِبْدأ التَّعَلُّم بأحْدث طُرُقِ التَدْرِيس** **تجربة مجانية لمدة أسبوع** **إِنْشَاء حِسَاب** **تَسْجِيل الدُّخُول** **🔴 جدول حصص مباشرة ** ## من نحن ؟ تَدْرِيس هي منصّة تعليميّة عبر الإنترنت في تونس موجّهة لتلامذة الابتدائي والأساسي والثّانوي، نقدّم دروس دعم في شكل فيديوهات مسجّلة لشرح كلّ دروس البرنامج الدّراسي وحصص مباشرة تفاعليّة تحت إشراف ثلّة من الأساتذة والمربّين من ذوي الخبرة من كامل أنحاء الجمهورية. اشترك الآن لِلتَّعَرُّف على طَرِيقَة تدْريسنَا والتَّأكُّد مِن مَدَى الإفادة التِي سَيكْتسبهَا أبْنائكم مِنَصة تَدرِيس تُمَكنكم مِن حِصص تجْريبيَّة مَجانِية. مِنَصة تَدرِيس تُؤْمِن أنَّ مهمتهَا الأخْذ بِيد كُلّ تِلْميذ مَهمَا كان مُستوَاهُ الدِّراسيُّ، لِتَمْكِينِهِ مِن أَخْذ نَسق مُعْتَدِل وَيجِد الوقْت لِلتَّدارك وَتطوِير مُستوَاه وتحْصيله العلْمِي. نُسَاعِد التِّلْميذ على التَّحْضير أو تَدارُك نَتائِج الثُّلاثيَّة اَلتِي لَم تَكُن فِي مُستَوَى التَّطلُّعات أو لِتثْبِيتِ المكْتسبات وَتطوِير المهارات. الأسْعار مَدرُوسة بِالنَّظر إِلى عَدد الحِصص والمَوادِّ شَهْريًّا مع إِمْكانيَّة التَّسْهيل فِي الدَّفْع و تُهْدِيك تَخفِيض عَلَى إشْتِرَاكَات أَفْرَادِ العَائِلَة الوَاحِدَة. فِي دَاركْ، تَنجم تَقْرَا دُونَ الحَاجَةِ إِلَى التَّنَقُّل... رَاحَتك وَرَاحَتْ صغِيرك و تَتَـفَادَى كُلُّ المَخَاطِر. ## **ماذا نقدّم ؟** توفّر منصّة تَدْرِيس عروضا متنوّعة للأق

tashfeen.tech
Fondateur / Founder
E-mail
Adresse / Address
WAFY BOUASSIDA

appt 5jd, rés diar el ons, Av slimen ben slimen, manar2, 
tunis
Activité Une plateforme de calcul des ouvrages 
génie civil en ligne permettant à l'ingénieur concepteur 
ou à l'étudiant de faire des simulations relatives à des 
modélisations courantes en ingénierie telle que les routes, 
les réservoirs, les ouvrages hydrauliques, etc. Geotashfeen.
tech se distingue par ses services considérés comme une 
première à l'international en Génie civil: Il n'existe aucune 
autre plateforme de calcul en ligne gratuite offrant la 
qualité et encore moins une telle multitude de services.
Aspect 
Technologique 
Plateforme 
web 
permettant 
à 
l'utilisateur de saisir des inputs de paramètres physiques 
et mécaniques puis cliquer sur un pushbutton pour 
obtenir ses résultats. Encore plus, l'utilisateur a la capacité 
d'imprimer gratuitement sa note de calcul. En front,la 
plateforme est écrite en js alors qu'en back elle est 
développée en php. geotashfeen.tech arrive à atteindre 
300 visiteurs par jours à savoir qu'elle a été lancée depuis 
début 2021.
Originalité geotashfeen.tech permet aux concepteurs 
de contourner l'utilisation des logiciels exe chers,lourds 
et crackés en échange de résultats très rapides et 
utilisables à l'échelle industrielle. En termes d'éducation, 
geotashfeen.tech est le berceau des étudiants curieux, 
novices et checheurs car pour chaque simulation, on peut 
télécharger la brochure relative au background théorique 
qui la régit. geotashfeen.tech est l'éducateur des 
étudiants du monde ainsi que le solveur des ingénieurs 
concepteurs.
Activity An online civil engineering calculation 
platform allowing the design engineer or student to 
perform simulations related to common engineering 
models such as roads, reservoirs, hydraulic structures, etc. 
Geotashfeen.tech stands out for its services which are 
considered as a first in the international civil engineering 
field: there is no other free online calculation platform 
offering such quality, let alone such a multitude of 
services.
Technological Aspect Web platform that allows the user to 
enter physical and mechanical parameters and then click 
on a pushbutton to get the results. Moreover, the user has 
the ability to print his calculation note for free. In front, 
the platform is written in js while in back it is developed in 
php. geotashfeen.tech can reach 300 visitors per day and 
has been launched since early 2021.
Originality Geotashfeen.tech allows designers to bypass 
the use of expensive, heavy and cracked exe software in 
exchange for very fast and usable results on an industrial 
scale. In terms of education, geotashfeen.tech is the 
cradle of curious, novice and research students because 
for each simulation, one can download the brochure 
related to the theoretical background that governs it. 
geotashfeen.tech is the educator of the world's students 
as well as the solver of design engineers.
FR
EN
Industry: Real Estate Tech
Country: Tunisia

Additional information: name: Trust IT description: Plateforme de gestion numérique offrant un marché de la réparation avec des services de réussite et de livraison pour les clients, basée sur un vaste réseau d'experts et de fournisseurs. industry: Tech Sercices/ engineering country: Tunisia website: entity_type: startup website_content: []( ProposEntreprisesParticuliersNous Contacter FR # La plateforme de gestion circulaire de vos actifs électroniques TrustiT aide les entreprises tunisiennes à suivre, maintenir, réparer et valoriser leurs équipements électroniques tout au long de leur cycle de vie — avec un impact carbone mesuré à chaque étape. Déjà actif en Tunisie depuis 2017. Nous ContacterDécouvrir nos solutions TrustiT aujourd'hui — à l'échelle régionale 2,940 Clients accompagnés (Tunisie, Arabie Saoudite, Qatar) 16 Experts et conseillers 339 Appareils reconditionnés 2 Partenariats gouvernementaux ## Nos solutions pour les entreprises Une plateforme unique pour gérer le cycle de vie complet de vos équipements électroniques — du suivi à la valorisation, en passant par la maintenance. ### Gestion d'actifs & impact carbone Suivez l'ensemble de votre parc électronique — enregistrement, attribution, historique de maintenance — et mesurez votre empreinte carbone à chaque étape du cycle de vie. En savoir plus ### Maintenance & réparation Centralisez les demandes de maintenance et de réparation de votre parc électronique professionnel, traitées par notre réseau de réparateurs certifiés en Tunisie. En savoir plus ### Durabilité & reconditionnement Donnez une seconde vie à vos équipements en fin de service grâce à notre filière de reconditionnement, et valorisez votre démarche de durabilité. En savoir plus Découvrez notre présence dans la région Qatar Arabie Saoudite ## Pourquoi choisir TrustiT ### Opérationnel en Tunisie Une plateforme qui fonctionne déjà avec des entreprises tunisiennes — suivi d'actifs, maintenance et reconditionnement, pas un concept à venir. ### Réseau certifié Des réparateurs vérifiés et formés, présents dans toute la Tunisie, pour une prise en charge rapide et fiable de votre parc. ### Impact mesuré Chaque action sur vos actifs — réparation, maintenance, retrait — est suivie et son impact carbone calculé, pour une démarche durable concrète. ## Ils nous accompagnent Des partenaires qui soutiennent le développement de TrustiT en Tunisie. De la rédaction des questions à l’analyse finale, laissez PollPro automatiser votre collecte d’avis Essayer gratuitement ### L’opinion de votre audience, décodée par l’IA. **En savoir plus sur nous** ###### Définissez votre Objectif (Prompt-to-Survey) _Ne créez plus de listes de questions. Tapez simplement votre besoin : « Je veux comprendre pourquoi les ventes de produits bio stagnent dans le sud-est malgré les promos. » Notre moteur LLM génère instantanément un sondage scientifiquement structuré, évitant les biais cognitifs et optimisé pour le taux de réponse._ ###### PollPro redéfinit le workflow de la donnée. Pourquoi perdre des heures à concevoir des questions quand une IA peut comprendre vos objectifs ? ###### Collecte Dynamique & Adaptative Fini l’ennui des formulaires linéaires. PollPro agit comme un enquêteur chevronné. Si un client mentionne un problème en caisse, l’IA rebondit : « Désolé de l’entendre. Était-ce lié à l’attente ou à un problème technique ? » Le résultat ? Une donnée 4x plus riche et un client qui se sent écouté, pas interrogé. ### PollPro : L'Intelligence au Cœur de Votre Réussite. Me

Activité Pour ajouter de la valeur aux fermes. On a 
un écosystème IoT composé d'un collier qui mesure en 
permanence la chaleur et la santé des bovins, ainsi q'un 
analyseur qui identifie la qualité et la teneur du lait. Notre 
modèle analysera les données sur le serveur pour avertir 
l'utilisateur en cas d'incident.
Aspect Technologique Les données sont transmises au 
serveur via LoRa. Le modèle d'IA reconnaît les patterns.
Originalité Un écosystème IoT évolutif pour les fermes 
intelligentes. 
La 
ferme 
est 
maintenant 
facilement 
accessible de partout.
Activity To add value to farms. We have an Internet of 
Things ecosystem consisting of a collar that continually 
measures the heat and health of the cattle, as well as an 
analyzer that identifies the quality and content of the milk. 
Our AI model will require the data on the server to warn 
the user in the case of an incident.
Technological Aspect Through LoRa, data is sent to the server. 
An AI model can spot patterns.
Originality A scalable IoT ecosystem for smart farms. The 
farm is now easily accessible from everywhere.

Dirigeants âgés de 6 à 18 ans ## Et si .. Vos enfants apprennent des compétences de vie, découvrent leur vrai potentiel et grandissent en confiance ? Voir nos formationsacheter une formation ## Réveillez l'entrepreneur chez votre enfant Youth Hub est un hub divertissant et engageant où nous proposons différents programmes et jeux éducatifs pour libérer le potentiel entrepreneurial des enfants et les immerger dans le monde entrepreneurial. Voir nos formationsacheter une formation ## pourquoi nos étudiants aiment apprendre avec nous ### Penser Apprenez à réfléchir aux défis existants et à comprendre la cause profonde des problèmes EmpathieEsprit critiqueConfiance en soi ### Concevoir Apprenez à concevoir des solutions créatives et à itérer à travers les tests Résolution de problèmeRéceptivitéTravail en équipe ### Changer Apprenez à diriger le changement et à planifier votre réussite Planificationdiscours publicAdaptabilité ## Commencez votre expérience entrepreneuriale ### Apprendre plus sur l'entrepreneuriat Une compréhension des concepts clés liés à la création d'entreprise. ### Développer ses compétences interpersonnelles Un processus d'apprentissage intense qui favorise le développement personnel. ### Vivre une expérience d'apprentissage unique Une approche d'apprentissage basée sur la pratique et l'expérimentation sans limites. ### Créer des idées innovantes Application de la créativité de votre enfant pour résoudre des problèmes du monde réel. ### Appartenir à une communauté solidaire Rencontres avec de nouveaux amis et travail en équipe. ### Être engagé dans le progrès de votre enfant Recevior un suivi détaillé du progrès de votre enfant avec des actions claires pour s'améliorer. Voir nos formationsacheter une formation Découvrir nos Événements ## Nos prochains Événements #### Nous offrons aux jeunes différents programmes d'entrepreneuriat pour mener le changement social dans leurs communautés Global Teenpreneur English Immersion Program 11-18 Ans Vivez une aventure extraordinaire aux États-Unis avec le programme d'échange Global Teenpreneur English Immersion Program ! ✈️ prenez le maintenant ! Activité Capxvalue est une solution digitale innovante 
qui s’adresse au demandeur de financement (entrepreneur, 
professionnel de la finance) comme au fournisseurs de 
financements (Banques, Fonds d'Investissements). 
Aspect Technologique Capxvalue est une plateforme web 
qui permet à l'utilisateur une fois loggé d'accéder à des 
fonctionnalités gratuites ou payantes. La programmation 
de l’interface client est basée sur JavaScript. Les 
technologies Apache, Symfony (PHP), Twig, Doctrine et 
Mysql sont utilisées coté serveur/base de donnée.
Originalité L'originalité de Capxvalue est qu'elle mutualise 
les états financiers historiques et previsionnels, de façon 
anonyme, par secteur, pour permettre à l'utilisateur de 
positionner son projet par rapport à d'autres comparables 
sectoriels (23 indicateurs). L'autre valeur ajoutée de 
Capxvalue est qu'elle permet à l'utilisateur d'estimer la 
valeur financière de son projet dans une perspective de 
levée de fonds ou bien de valorisation periodique d'un 
portefeuille d'actifs.
Activity Capxvalue is an innovative digital solution 
that targets the financing applicant (entrepreneur, finance 
professional) as well as the financing provider (banks, 
investment funds).
Technological Aspect Capxvalue is a web platform that 
allows the user, once logged in, to access free or paid 
features. The programming of the client interface is based 
on JavaScript. Apache, Symfony (PHP), Twig, Doctrine 
and Mysql technologies are used on the server/database 
side.
Originality The originality of Capxvalue is that it 
pools historical and forecasted financial statements, 
anonymously, by sector, to allow the user to position 
his project in relation to other comparable sectors (23 
indicators). The other added value of Capxvalue is 
that it allows the user to estimate the financial value of 
his project in a perspective of fund raising or periodic 
valuation of an asset portfolio.

EN

Additional information: name: Africa Eats description: A publicly listed holding company that invests in and supports a portfolio of African food and agriculture companies, aiming to reduce post‑harvest losses, increase smallholder farmer incomes and move Africa towards net food export. country: Mauritius website: linkedin: entity_type: investor external_profiles.website: external_profiles.linkedin: social_media.linkedin: website_content: # []( * Home * Portfolio * How it Works * Invest * Blog * Podcasts ## Stories of for-profit solutions to hunger and poverty Read ## Guided Tours Read ## Invest Read ## How it Works Read ## Portfolio Read ## Berkshire Africa Read ## Stories of for-profit solutions to hunger and poverty Read ## Guided Tours Read ## Invest Read ## How it Works Read ## Portfolio Read ## Berkshire Africa Read ### Africa Eats Despite over ¾ of Africans being **farmers** or **children of farmers** , not every African is able to eat three meals per day. Most of that is due to post-harvest losses, with up to ⅓ of the grain and almost ½ of all the fruit and vegetables grown never making it to a plate to eat. Because of the post-harvest losses, Africa spends **tens of billions of dollars** per year **importing food**. Ending this downward spiral of mounting debt ends when Africa is a net exporter of food. There is **far too much friction** in **funding** the existing, homegrown, for-profit **solutions to hunger and poverty** too often ignored by financial institutions. Not just initial funding, but growth-stage funding and critical financial services. A solution for all these issues is **Africa Eats** , a holding company with a diverse set of African food/ag companies supporting hundreds of thousands of **smallholder farmers** , feeding millions of Africans. Africa Eats does not try solving this problem from scratch, but instead begins with **two dozenbizi**, who were all once tiny SMEs, who in 2025 earned **over $56 million** in aggregate revenues, **over $8 million** in aggregate profits, and which increased the incomes of more than 1 million smallholder farmers and their families. Entity type: investor
Name: Africa Finance Corporation
Description: Africa Finance Corporation (AFC), is an investment-grade multilateral finance institution established in 2007 through a Treaty among several sovereign African states, to be the catalyst for private-sector-led infrastructure investment across Africa. AFC’s investment approach combines specialist industry expertise with a focus on financial and technical advisory, project structuring, project development and risk capital to address Africa’s infrastructure development needs and drive sustainable economic growth. AFC invests in high quality infrastructure assets that provide essential services in the core infrastructure sectors of power, natural resources, heavy industry, transport and telecommunications. To date, the Corporation has invested approximately US$4.5 billion in projects within 28 countries across North, East, West and Southern Africa.

May 18, 2026 1 Share []( Aboyeji, founding partner of Future Africa []( _** For the best experience, download the free Africa Private Equity News appAndroid**|**iOS **_ * * * **Africa Finance Corporation (AFC) has approved a commitment of up to $100 million to invest in Africa-focused technology fund managers.** Through this commitment, AFC will deploy catalytic capital in leading Africa-focused technology funds and in particular African-owned fund managers. In doing so, AFC aims to address the underrepresentation of local capital in venture funding by catalysing greater participation from African institutional investors and deepening local ownership within the ecosystem. Samaila Zubairu, president and CEO of AFC said: “Across the continent, young Africans are not waiting for the digital economy to arrive; they are seizing the moment – adopting technology, creating markets and solving real economic problems faster than infrastructure has kept pace. That is the investment signal. AFC’s $100 million Africa-focused Technology Fund will accelerate the convergence of growing demand, rapid technology adoption, youthful demographics and the enabling infrastructure we are building. Digital infrastructure is now as fundamental to Africa’s transformation as roads, rail, ports and power – enabling productivity, payments, logistics, services, data and cross-border trade, while creating jobs and industrial scale.” As part of the initial deployment, AFC has made anchor commitments to Lightrock Africa Fund II and Future Africa Fund III,

Entity type: investor
Name: Africa Insight Advisors
Description: Africa Insight Advisors (AIA) is a Tanzania based (although we have one staff member in Kenya) management consulting and investment advisory business that works throughout East Africa. Simply put, we are obsessively focused on helping private companies expand, be more efficient, modernize, attract capital, and otherwise grow. On the consulting side, we work with both foreign and local companies. We assist multinationals interested in entering East Africa, and help them understand the operating environment and where they fit in. Our work runs the gamut, from feasibility studies and competitive assessments, to market analysis, regulatory overviews, and strategic entry planning. Given a complex and difficult operating environment, we provide the local knowledge to cut costs, clear hurdles, and make a launch in East Africa as easy as possible. For local companies, we offer a host of change management services designed to make stronger companies, and turn a first mover advantage into a competitive advantage. We work to help businesses modernize and adopt international and industry best practices, diversify into new lines of business, develop turn‑around strategies, and improve efficiency. Finally, in the investment side, we help local companies secure capital (debt and equity) from international financiers. Our role is dictated by the client, and can be as all encompassing as managing the whole process‑from document creation to due diligence and deal close; to simply linking clients with potential investors.
Industry: Investment Banking, Strategic Advisory
Country: Tanzania
City: Dar es Salaam
Investment focus: Corporate Finance, Debt Advisory, Mergers & Acquisitions, Strategic Advisory

Africa Tech Summit Nairobi 2025 Announces 10 Ventures for Investment Showcase – Over 200 submissions were received from more than 24 countries, including Burundi, Côte d’Ivoire, Democratic Republic of Congo, Egypt, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, Ghana, Zambia, Mali, Morocco, Mozambique, South Africa, Tanzania, Chad, and Rwanda. The 10 ventures, representing sectors such as fintech, insurtech, healthtech, agritech, and cleantech, were chosen for their innovative ideas and potential to drive significant impact across the continent. Recently released data from the startup funding tracker Africa: The Big Deal reveals that fintech accounted for nearly half of all startup funding in 2024. Among the four largest fundraisers was Moniepoint, which secured $110 million in a Series C round, valuing the company at over $1 billion and making it Africa’s eighth Unicorn. While the African startup ecosystem has experienced a slump in recent years, funding activities are projected to recover in 2025, driven by innovative startups and growing investor interest. The selected ventures will present their solutions to industry leaders, entrepreneurs, and global investors. Dojah (Nigeria) is an anti-fraud infrastructure platform that enables businesses to detect, prevent, and manage fraudulent activities in real-time. Duhqa (Kenya) is a B2B platform that connects retailers with manufacturers and suppliers, locally and internationally. They aim to solve logistics and delivery inefficiencies by enabling retailers to get their goods delivered conveniently and affordably. Flow Global (Uganda) is addressing the significant problem faced by Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in developing countries: the lack of access to adequate financing that hinders their growth potential. Bloomberg and the African Development Bank Group Launch Partnership to Catalyze Private Investment Across Africa September 24, 2025 **NEW YORK, NY September 24 –** Michael R. Bloomberg, Founder of Bloomberg L.P. and Bloomberg Philanthropies and Chair of the Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero (GFANZ), and Dr. Sidi Ould Tah, President of the African Development Bank Group (AfDB), today announced a new partnership to mobilize private sector investment across the African continent. “Africa has enormous opportunities for economic growth and innovation, and this new partnership with the African Development Bank will help to unlock more of it by reducing barriers to private investment,” **said****Michael R. Bloomberg, Founder of Bloomberg L.P. and Bloomberg Philanthropies and Chair of GFANZ.** The announcement was made at the Bloomberg Philanthropies Global Forum, where Dr. Sidi Ould Tah and Mike Bloomberg outlined plans to convene leading private financial institutions together with the AfDB to unlock significantly greater flows of private capital for Africa’s growth and resilience. “Today we took a decisive step–embarking on a new journey of partnerships and collective action. Through our four cardinal points of strategic priorities, we have affirmed our commitment to work closely with the private sector and our international partners to create a financial framework that serves Africa better–and on its own terms,”**said Dr. Sidi Ould Tah, President of the AfDB.** “I am glad that we have allies who believe in the importance of progressive partnerships and who are ready to work hand in hand with the African Development Bank Group. Together we will consolidate our strengths for mobilizing private sector resources and unleashing investments at scale for Africa’s development.” Africa requires hundreds of billions of dollars in investment annually to strengthen sustainable economic growth, improve livelihoods, adapt to climate-related impacts, and achieve development goals. This will require mobilizing capital from a wide range of private sector investors and financial institutions, both African and international. The AfDB has long partnered with private sector investors to mobilize investment, and under Dr. Tah’s leadership, has continued to explore how to scale and improve risk-sharing mechanisms and innovati

Successful pan-African investor Verdant Capital shares some of their experiences and insights of the African Investments Dashboard and investment landscape. More than 1,500 senior-level attendees from institutional investors, private equity and venture capital funds, DFIs, family offices, and top African executives are set to gather in London next month for AFSIC – Investing in Africa, the premier event for Driving Investment and Business Growth Across the African Continent. “AFSIC is highly focused on the matching of live investment opportunities,” says Rupert McCammon, founder and Managing Director of AFSIC. “Prior to the event, we will distribute our renowned Deal Book containing over 300+ investable African opportunities. Investors can engage directly with project promoters via the African Investments Dashboard, the continent’s leading digital platform for sourcing private sector deals, or through our user-friendly Event and Meeting App.” Now in its 12th year, AFSIC connects Africa’s most promising businesses with global investors through industry-specific and insight-driven sessions, country-focused investment summits, exclusive networking sessions, and ever-popular "Meet the Investor" sessions designed to fast-track deal flow across the continent. “The investment environment in Africa offers prospects for growth and development, propelled by demographic shifts, technological progress, and investments in infrastructure,” says Verdant Capital, a leading investment bank and investment manager operating on a pan-African basis that has used the African Investments Dashboard and is a returning sponsor for AFSIC – Investing in Africa 2025. “From the viewpoint of microfinance, we have explored substantial opportunities in East Africa - Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania – as well as in West Africa, particularly in Nigeria and Ghana, and in Southern Africa – notably in Zambia and South Africa.” Verdant Capital says it’s had an excellent experience with the African Investments Dashboard from the perspective of microfinance, as a pan-African investor engaging with microfinanc

Additional information: name: Albatros Energy Mali S.A. description: Developer, constructor, owner and operator of the first Independent Private Power Project (IPP) in Mali, a 90 MW thermal power plant. organization_type: Power Project Developer country: Mali city: Kayes website: linkedin: entity_type: investor website_content: * Home * About Us * Corporate Culture * Our Sponsors * Our Shareholders * Our Board of Directors * Our Management Team * The Power Plant * Unique elements & Firsts * Location * Technology * Environmental * Our Partners * The Lenders * The Facility Agent * The Security Agent * CSR * News-Press * Gallery * []( Select Page * Home * About Us * Corporate Culture * Our Sponsors * Our Shareholders * Our Board of Directors * Our Management Team * The Power Plant * Unique elements & Firsts * Location * Technology * Environmental * Our Partners * The Lenders * The Facility Agent * The Security Agent * CSR * News-Press * Gallery * []( PreviousNext 12 **Albatros Energy Mali** **S.A**(AEM) independent private power project is a 90 megawatt (MW) heavy fuel oil power station built in Kayes, western Mali. It is Mali’s first IPP to feed into the national grid. The power station consists of 6 Caterpillar diesel reciprocating engines. The project achieved financial close in June 2017. Construction commenced in July 2017, and was commissioned in October 31st 2018, ahead of schedule. It is providing a much needed sustainable energy source allowing an increased access to electricity for Mali’s citizens and enabling industries to grow. We are excited to work together with our stakeholders in making a beneficial contribution to Mali’s power sector and supporting the Government in its sustainable development and energy poverty reduction efforts. # MW installed capacity # MONTHS construction period # € millions investment # MW installed capacity # MONTHS construction period # € millions investment ## Unique Elements & Firsts  First IPP in Mali. Proven to be ground breaking for other energy projects in Mali and vital for the development of the renewable sector, which needs base load for stabilising the grid.  The first Islamic financing (Ijara) for a private project in a francophone and sub-saharan African jurisdiction governed by French Law in combination with conventional financing. Financed by a currency mix of Euros and FCFA.  First IPP in Francophone Africa where the debt service reserve account is guaranteed by a partial guarantee provided by GuarantCo.  Malian ownership combined with international know how: the local involvement and local partnerships are a key reason for the success of this project which combines the skills and experience of a local businessman (Amadou Sow), an international energy expert (Koenraad Beckers) and an experienced project finance expert (Ruth Beckers).  First post-conflict infrastructure project – crucial for the economic development and energy poverty reduction. First significant infrastructure project after the conflict in 2012.

The firm has a recognised expertise in OHADA business law and in the energy, mining, infrastructure and finance sectors. A&P advises international institutions and corporations, investment funds, private and public companies in the structuring and management of their projects in Cameroon and Africa, with a particular focus on the OHADA zone. A&P has its main office in Yaoundé, Cameroon, and another office in Paris, France. A&P is a truly international firm which stands out for the quality and excellence of its services and has a strong reputation in the energy, mining, infrastructure and financing sectors. The firm advice and assistance are based on an in-depth knowledge of the legal, regulatory and business environments in Africa, with a full understanding of its clients’ business objectives. A&P is a fast-growing law firm, devoted to the African continent which aims at supporting projects with a high impact on African economies. The firm is really innovative in its flexible hierarchical structure which provides greater efficiency and responsiveness. The cohesion of its staff and the prevailing team spirit is a true competitive edge. A&P also has a strong commitment towards social responsibility and implements best practices to promote diversity within the firm. The Fund’s goal is to support the growth and development of innovative businesses that are poised to disrupt traditional markets and drive economic growth in the region. The Fund's focus is on investing in startups that have the potential to create significant social and economic impact, as well as generate high financial returns for its investors. The fund is managed by Angaza Capital. Investment Strategy The Fund will invest in tech-enabled startups with a focus on growth-stage companies that have a proven product or service and have demonstrated traction in their respective markets. The Fund’s target investment size usually ranges from $250,000 to $2 million, with a typical hold period of 5-7 years. The Fund will consider technology startups in ESG-friendly sectors. Geographic Focus The Fund invests primarily in startups based in East Africa, with a focus on Rwanda. The Fund’s portfolio is comprised of 3 categories: 1. Rwanda Based: headquarter of the company is in Rwanda or it has operations in Rwanda. 2. Regional/East Africa focus: headquarter of the company is in any country of the East African Community (EAC) or it has operations in the EAC. 3. Tech-transfer from international companies setting up local operators & companies based in the wider African market. Investment Criteria a. Stage Focus on growth stage companies (post early stage and before maturity that allows them to access banking industry and capital markets funding). The investment are done at seed round to B-round .b. Company traction and metrics ● Companies that can demonstrate yearly revenue between U$50,000 and U$5 million. ● Companies should be able to show momentum in revenue, regulatory approvals, impressive awards with unique validation, IP or patented innovation. ● Companies must have the ability to scale across borders. c. Management Team The fund prioritises companies with a strong and experienced management team, who have a track record

The PE firm raised $1.23 billion for Apis Global Growth Fund III and Apis Growth Markets Fund III, excluding co-investment commitments, it said in a statement. The two vehicles, collectively its third fund, closed 23% above the PE firm’s initial target. About 70% of its existing limited partners committed to half of the corpus, Apis said. Prior to starting Ascent, Eric Greenberg ran Leveraged Finance for Goldman Sachs in Asia ex-Japan and was responsible for raising billions of dollars in buy-outs and pre-IPO financings. This team and network ultimately became Ascent. # Private Markets? Ascent focuses on unlisted and private investment opportunities. Additional information: name: Association Tunisienne des Investisseurs en Capital description: L’ATIC fédère et représente les opérateurs Tunisiens du Capital investissement ce qui lui confère une forte légitimité en qualité de porte-parole de la profession auprès des pouvoirs publics. organization_type: private equity firm country: South Africa city: Cape Town, Johannesburg website: linkedin: entity_type: investor external_profiles.website: external_profiles.linkedin: social_media.linkedin: social_media.facebook: social_media.instagram: social_media.twitter: social_media.youtube: website_content: [ []( Pas de résultat Voir tous les résultats * Business * Eco * Executives * Banking * Startups * Biz’art & Cult * Vidéo * Podcast * Our Events * Femmes Entrepreneurs Tunisie * Africa Means Business * AFRICAN ESG SUMMIT * Packs TRE * Inscrivez-vous * Business * Eco * Executives * Banking * Startups * Biz’art & Cult * Vidéo * Podcast * Our Events * Femmes Entrepreneurs Tunisie * Africa Means Business * AFRICAN ESG SUMMIT * Packs TRE * Inscrivez-vous []( []( Pas de résultat Voir tous les résultats # Capital-investissement en Tunisie: le grand retour de l’ATIC comme levier de la transformation économique 18 juin 2026 Dans Business Après 8 ans d’absence, la conférence annuelle de l’Association tunisienne des investisseurs en capital (ATIC) a signé un retour remarqué en juin 2026 à Tunis. Sous la présidence de Selma Ben Hamida, l’événement ne s’est pas limité à une reprise de calendrier. Dans son intervention, la présidente a insisté sur le fait qu’il s’agissait d’un “acte délibéré”, celui d’un secteur qui choisit de reprendre pleinement sa place dans le débat économique et institutionnel après une période de turbulences. Elle a rappelé que le capital-investissement n’est pas un acteur financier comme les autres, mais un mécanisme de transformation économique reposant sur l’apport en fonds propres et un accompagnement stratégique des entreprises. Un rôle qui, selon elle, devient d’autant plus essentiel dans un contexte où les besoins de restructuration et de développement restent importants. Les chiffres présentés lors de la conférence illustrent cette dynamique. En 2025, le secteur a mobilisé 709 MDT de fonds levés et investi 740 MDT dans l’économie tunisienne. Plus de 223 entreprises ont été financées, permettant la création ou le maintien de plus de 5 800 emplois. La présidente a également souligné la montée en puissance du capital-retournement, devenu un axe central du secteur, représentant près de 40% des investissements totaux, contre 30% trois ans auparavant, avec 204 MDT consacrés aux opérations de restructuration. Mais ce tableau positif est accompagné d’une alerte claire. L’ATIC a mis en garde contre l’échéance, prévue fin 2026, des incitations fiscales mises en place par la loi de 2019. Ces dispositifs, qui facilitent l’orientation de capitaux privés vers les entreprises en difficulté, sont jugés essentiels pour la stabilité du financement de l’économie réelle. L’associ

Name: Barak Fund Management Ltd
Description: Barak seeks to generate equity-like returns in a capital-constrained market with relatively low volatility and limited correlation to the broader markets. Strategy focuses on fully-funded or blended debt in the African commodity markets, using asset-backed loans with various forms of collateral.
Country: Mauritius
City: Floreal
Investment focus: African commodity markets, Asset-backed loans, Soft agricultural and food-related sectors, Mining, energy and other commodity sectors
Additional information: name: Barak Fund Management Ltd description: Barak seeks to generate equity-like returns in a capital-constrained market with relatively low volatility and limited correlation to the broader markets. Strategy focuses on fully-funded or blended debt in the African commodity markets, using asset-backed loans with various forms of collateral. organization_type: Fund Management country: Mauritius city: Floreal website: linkedin: entity_type: investor website_content: * Home * About Us * Lending * Portfolio * Impact * Press * Contact Contact # The Barak Story Barak has grown into Africa’s leading alternative credit financier since 2008 ) ## Investors Barak Fund Management, launched in 2008, has built up a track record of 11 years in African alternative credit financing and to date has provided funding of over US$5bn to a variety of SME businesses in more than 30 countries across the continent. Barak has African origination and fund advisory offices in Mauritius, South Africa, Kenya, Ghana, Ivory Coast and the UK, and has 25 on-the-ground investment specialists focusing on a variety of commodity sectors. It provides a variety of funding structures including both Impact, Development and Shariah-compliant funding. Barak manages 7 credit funds focusing on SME commodity trade finance and infrastructure development with a total AUM in excess of US$1.4bn to date. Barak provides fund management solutions to a range of global investors and funding to an extensive list of counterparties throughout Africa, working with direct producers and farmers, as well as traders offering a broad network across the continent. Barak’s core investment rationale is related primarily to the soft agricultural and food-related sectors, though the flagship STF Fund does also finance the mining, energy and other commodity-related sectors. The Barak investment approach is based upon the principles of discipline, diversification, collateralization and downside-case scenario valuation. The company acknowledges that African investments encompass a certain degree of risk given the nature of investing in a continent whose primary markets are influenced by a multitude of volatile factors. Each investment is thus approached with a stringent on-boarding process – using both desktop and on-the-ground due-diligence processes in order to determine the viability of a potential project’s funding. For more information, visit www.barakfund.com ## An initiative by * []( * []( * []( * []( * * []( ## Signatory of * * All rights reserved. website_metadata.url: website_metadata.content_length: 3643 contact.primary_social: social_media.linkedin: linkedin_url: external_profiles.website: external_profiles.linkedin: external_profiles.wellfound: aum: US$1.4bn normalized_name: barak fund management ltd

Sign in * News * News Feed * Editorials * Events * Podcasts * Webinars # Belgian Investment Company for Developing Countries BIO – quest for balance between profit and worldwide development Sep 16, 2019 _**The Belgian Investment Company for Developing Countries BIO is a European Development Finance Institution (DFI) focusing on direct and indirect investments in Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) across the world.**_ The company pays particular attention to Belgian Development Cooperation partner countries and to less developed countries. Last year, its investment portfolio expanded by 22 new projects, amounting to 150 million EUROS and thus an impressive total of 147 projects, worth over 750 million EUROS, was reached. This article raises the curtain on BIO’s investment activity and its impact. At BIO, development projects are encouraged within the framework of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) adopted by the United Nations in 2015. Its current investments contribute to 6 out of 17 SDGs but, depending on the sector, the company can support additional goals. Right now, the funding institution contributes to the goals of ending poverty in all its forms, achieving gender equality, affordable and clean energy, local economic growth, infrastructure, innovation and reduced inequalities within and among countries. Cartesian Wealth and Advisory At Cartesian Wealth & Advisory, we don’t just manage wealth; we cultivate prosperity. Let’s embark on this mathematical adventure together. ### OUR STORY Our name pays homage to René Descartes, the visionary philosopher and mathematician who famously declared, “I think, therefore I am.” Descartes’ legacy resonates through our firm, guiding our commitment to thoughtful problem-solving and pioneering insights. Our team of financial experts don’t settle for surface-level answers. We delve deep, analysing, questioning, collaborating and refining until we find the optimal path. ### OUR VALUES **Integrity** : We uphold the highest standards of honesty and transparency in all our dealings, ensuring that your trust in us is well-placed. **Personalised Service** : Each client is unique, and so is our approach. We take the time to understand your individual financial goals and craft tailored solutions to achieve them. **Expertise** : Our team of skilled professionals is dedicated to staying ahead of the curve with in-depth market knowledge and innovative strategies. ### OUR APPROACH At Cartesian Wealth & Advisory, we believe that wealth management is an art form—a delicate balance of precision and vision. Our team of seasoned specialists, each with a focused competency in the financial sector, collaborates to tailor bespoke, long-term solutions for our clients. We don’t settle for mass-market approaches; instead, we provide individual attention and personalized strategies that align with your unique goals. * * * ## Wealth Management * **Clarity of Thought** : Just as Descartes sought clarity in philosophy we bring clarity to your financial journey. Our transparent communication ensures you understand every step we take as we seek elegant solutions to your strategies. * **Tailored Solutions** : One size doesn’t fit all. Your goals are unique – so are our strategies. We craft custom solutions that resonate with your aspirations, whether you’re building wealth, planning for retirement, or preserving your legacy. * **Personalised Service & Dedicated Advisors**: Say goodbye to chatbots and call centres. Our dedicated wealth managers become your trusted partners – by your side throughout your financial journey, understanding your dreams, fears and aspirations. * **Prosperity Through Planning** : Descartes’ maxim —“I think, therefore I am” — becomes our mantra: “I plan, therefore I prosper.” Let us guide you toward

Suspendisse varius enim in eros elementum tristique. ButtonButton #### At Catalyst Fund, we are committed to investing in a climate-resilient future for Africa. As a venture capital fund and venture builder, we partner with visionary founders who are developing high-impact climate adaptation solutions to enhance the resilience of communities and the planet. Our investments span critical sectors including climate-smart agritech and food systems, climate insurance, land restoration, cold chain, waste management, clean energy, and water management. What sets us apart is our active, hands-on venture-building approach at the pre-seed stage. Drawing from our extensive experience as founders and operators in emerging markets, we support startups with their tech, product, data, and marketing challenges, propelling them towards product-market fit and rapid growth. Catalyst Fund began its journey in 2015 as a global accelerator managed by BFA Global, targeting early-stage inclusive fintech startups dedicated to improving the financial health of underserved communities across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Our inaugural fund, supported by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation and JPMorgan Chase & Co., and fiscally sponsored by Rockefeller Philanthropy Advisors, successfully backed 20 startups across 15 emerging markets. In 2019, we scaled our accelerator with support from the UK Foreign Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), PayPal, and Mastercard Foundation, expanding our reach to 36 additional inclusive tech startups in Nigeria, Kenya, South Africa, Ghana, India, and Mexico. He co-founded Clarmondial with Tanja Havemann in 2010, to design and implement investment solutions within the natural resource management sectors. Fred also acted as Chief Investment Officer of IFIT Fund Services from 2013 to 2016. Prior to joining IFIT, he was the fund manager of responsAbility Ventures I, a venture capital fund for businesses that address basic needs of low-income population in emerging markets. During his nearly 5 years at responsAbility, Fred led equity and quasi-equity investments in multiple sectors and continents, heading a team that analysed some 2’000 SMEs and microfinance institutions. He worked in investment banking (Mergers & Acquisitions) in South America from 1999 to 2006, before earning an MBA at London Business School, and leading climate-related investments at Climate Change Capital, a London-based asset manager. Swiss and Brazilian, Fred is a native Portuguese speaker, is fluent in English and Spanish, speaks French and is learning German. Looking for love? Click here to join the HIV+ dating Network in Nigeria.** # Full-Stack Developer at Cowry Asset Management Limited * **⏲** Sep 3, 2025, 7:36 AM * **⋕** View all ICT Support & Telecommunications jobs * * * Cowry Asset Management Limited is a leading investment banking firm licensed by the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our goal is to provide tailored investment solutions to clients across diverse sectors. We are recruiting to fill the position below: **Job Title:** Full-Stack Developer **Location:** Lagos Job type: Full-time **About the Job** * We are seeking a Full-Stack Developer to join our technology team. The successful candidate will play a vital role in building and maintaining enterprise-grade applications that support our financial and corporate operations. * We are looking for a developer who is not only technically strong in C#, ASP.NET Core, and SQL but also creative, solution-driven, and open to exploring the best tools and frameworks to achieve business goals. **Responsibilities** * Design and develop full-stack applications with ASP.NET Core, C#, and SQL Server as the primary stack. * Build and maintain RESTful APIs to support system integration and automation. * Develop responsive and user-friendly front-end interfaces using HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, and JavaScript frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue). * Design and optimize relational database schemas. * Write and tune SQL queries, stored procedures, and views. * Ensure data integrity, performance, and scalability. * Work with automated build and deployment pipelines to ensure smooth delivery of applications. * Monitor applications for performance, security, and reliability. * Stay up-to-date with industry trends and modern frameworks. * Proactively suggest process improvements and system enhancements. * Apply creativity and analytical thinking to solve business challenges. * Work closely with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver effective solutions. * Actively contribute to architecture discussions and planning sessions. * Participate in peer code reviews and promote best practices. * Write clean, maintainable, and testable code. * Conduct unit and integration testing. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field. * Over 3 years of proven experience as a Full-Stack Developer. * Strong expertise with C#, ASP.NET Core, and SQL Server. * Understanding of software engineering best practices (OOP, SOLID, design patterns)

We treat every client as a partner, working together to move money – confidently, compliantly, and without compromise. Driven by our mission to support prosperity in hard-to-reach regions, Crown Agents Bank provides vital financial services through specialist FX, cross-border payments, trade finance, and treasury management work. Our relationship-led model, global market access and deep regional expertise means we can facilitate the two-way flow of funds throughout the world’s most complex financial markets. We deliver prosperity in markets we serve, and do so guided by our values of integrity, partnership, and innovation. We are committed to operating transparently and ethically, forging strong relationships with our clients, and continually seeking new ways to drive positive change in the financial sector. Entity type: investor
Name: Crown Agents Investment Management
Description: Acuity Ventures is a venture capital firm that invests in early to growth-stage technology companies in Africa. Acuity leverages its partners' strong investment track record in technology and deep data science expertise to capture and use information to inform investment decisions and help investee companies realize outsized growth. Acuity's data-driven strategy allows it to recognize and amplify product-market fit, providing a more accurate quantitative view to early stage-investing at a company stage when accounting metrics are a less reliable predictor of company success. Acuity focuses on enterprise, soft infrastructure, and platforms that integrate Africa.

Additional information: name: Emerging Africa Infrastructure Fund description: A PIDG company that provides senior debt products for infrastructure projects in sub-Saharan countries. country: Mauritius city: London website: linkedin: entity_type: investor external_profiles.website: external_profiles.linkedin: external_profiles.dealroom: website_content: × * Who we are * Our board * Fund Manager & Team * The Private Infrastructure Development Group * What we do * Sector Focus * Our code of conduct * Disclosures * Complaints and Whistleblowing Policy * Legal Statements * Projects * Our impact * News and Media CONTACT US []( * * * Contact us * Who we are * Our board * Fund Manager & Team * The Private Infrastructure Development Group * What we do * Sector Focus * Our code of conduct * Disclosures * Complaints and Whistleblowing Policy * Legal Statements * Projects * Our impact * News and Media Home » What we do » **Our impact** # Our impact Our investments enable the delivery of climate-resilient, inclusive infrastructure projects that transform economies and improve lives in Africa and Asia. Their impact can often be felt for decades, by people and businesses far beyond the original project location. Above all, quality infrastructure enables people and businesses to plan for the future with confidence. The projects we fund contribute to political and economic stability, strengthen regional integration and trade, build resilience and human capital and stimulate productivity and growth. EAAIF is creating access to low carbon infrastructure and taking action on both mitigation and adaptation to accelerate African industrialisation and close the energy access gap, whilst supporting the global transition to net zero. We have invested $500m in renewables and generated over 1000MW of clean energy. We invest with a gender lens to optimise impact for women and girls and we are increasingly incorporating Equality, Diversity and Inclusion criteria into our investment strategy to be more intentional about the impact we create for the most marginalised communities. Our approach is guided by the Private Infrastructure Development Group Theory of Change and is designed to make a significant contribution towards the attainment of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. We are one group with a single mission. We get infrastructure finance moving and multiplying – accelerating climate action and sustainable development **Fig. 1:** _PIDG Theory of Change_ _We measure our impact at project level in terms of:_ **People impacted** – 147.7 million people are benefitting from EAAIF-funded projects **Jobs created** – directly and indirectly through the value chain; short and long term; human capital development through skills-building **Fiscal contributions** – directly and indirectly through the value chain Improved access to infrastructure in terms of affordability and inclusion (e.g. people with disabilities or other marginalised communities) Advances in gender equality Climate resil

Additional information: name: Manufacturing Africa description: Manufacturing Africa supports green manufacturing start-ups in Africa with strategy, investor access, and fully funded transaction advisory services to accelerate sustainable manufacturing across the continent. country: Kenya website: linkedin: entity_type: investor website_content: Skip to main content Menu * About Us * Mission * Team * Support * For Manufacturing, Service, Enabling Businesses * For Investors * For Start-Ups * Countries * Kenya * Ethiopia * Nigeria * Rwanda * Senegal * Uganda * Tanzania * Insights * Case Studies * Reports * Impact * More * MA News * Events * Programme Documents * Careers * Frequently Asked Questions * Contact Us * linkedin # Fundraising # Toolkit Launch Officially launching on the 23rd of July at 6pm 23 July 2026 * 18 days * 14 hours * 14 minutes * 47 seconds ## Accelerating sustainable ## manufacturing across Africa []( #### Start Ups We support green manufacturing start-ups in Africa with strategy and investor access to scale impact to grow the green manufacturing ecosystem. []( #### Manufacturers We provide fully funded transaction advisory services to fast-track investment and help secure the capital you need for expansion. []( #### Investors Discover high-potential, de-risked manufacturing ventures ready for investment and get first access to growth engines across the continent. #### IMPACT AT A GLANCE 280 + Deals supportedor in progress £ 2B Billion FDI mobilized 57 Deals reached financial close 150 k Direct & indirect jobs committed _(last updated in April 2026)_ #### IMPACT STORIES ### A vibrant manufacturing environment, unlocking extensive opportunities Africa’s population is projected to double by 2050, and the working population is expected to increase by 800 million. Africa will need to create approximately 18 million jobs annually to provide sufficient employment opportunities for this population. The manufacturing sector has the potential to provide millions of better and more inclusive jobs across the continent, while building resilient economies. Over the past five years, Manufacturing Africa has been dedicated to unlocking this potential. Supported by UKaid from the UK government, we provide expert assistance that turns ambition into tangible, sustainable growth. # 5 # Years of Manufacturing in Africa #### DEALS CLOSED SPOTLIGHT Supporting Ethiopia’s Food Security Ethiopia's largest consumer goods platform secured a £17.25 million investment to build a new edible oil plant. This major expansion will create over 200 new jobs and support the livelihoods of up to 7,000 smallholder farmers through local sourcing of raw materials. _Read More_ []( Growing a Kenyan Household Name Glacier Products, home to the popular Dairyland brand, secured **£4.93 million** to expand its facilities and diversify its product range. This investment will create **50 direct and 500 indirect jobs** while strengthening the local dairy farming community._Read More_ []( Boosting Pharm

Entity type: investor
Name: Nigeria Infrastructure Debt Fund
Description: The Nigeria Infrastructure Debt Fund (NIDF) is Nigeria’s, and Africa’s, first and only listed infrastructure debt fund, which provides long-term local currency financing to commercially viable infrastructure projects and businesses located in Nigeria. NIDF seeks to provide its unitholders regular, predictable and sustainable income, while preserving the capital value of their investment over the long term. It provides investors exposure to infrastructure debt by investing in senior and subordinated debt of projects and businesses that provide economically and socially critical infrastructure services to their users.
Country: Nigeria
Investment focus: Infrastructure debt
Additional information: name: Nigeria Infrastructure Debt Fund description: The Nigeria Infrastructure Debt Fund (NIDF) is Nigeria’s, and Africa’s, first and only listed infrastructure debt fund, which provides long-term local currency financing to commercially viable infrastructure projects and businesses located in Nigeria. Entity type: investor
Name: Norfund
Description: Norfund is the development finance institution (DFI) of the Norwegian government. Norfund aims to provide debt and equity in countries where the private sector is weak and access to capital is scarce. This is primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa, but also in some countries in South East Asia and Central America. Norfund's priority sectors are Clean energy, Financial institutions, Green Infrastructure and Scalable Enterprises. Norfund also invests in some specialised Funds to support small and medium sized companies in developing countries.
Investment focus: Clean energy, Financial institutions, Green infrastructure, Scalable enterprises

Additional information: name: Numeral description: Numeral XII is a South African‑founded investment holding group with a growing presence across Africa, Europe, and Asia, specialising in fintech, biotechnology, and asset management to drive sustainable growth and long‑term value creation. country: South Africa city: Pretoria website: linkedin: entity_type: investor website_content: Skip to main content * Home * Shared Services * SEM & SENS * About * Brands * Careers * Bond Program * Contact * Home * Shared Services * SEM & SENS * About * Brands * Careers * Bond Program * Contact # STRATEGIC GROWTH SOLUTIONS THE FUTURE OF FINTECH AND BIOTECH ## ABOUT NUMERAL Numeral XII is a South African–founded investment holding group with a growing presence across Africa, Europe, and Asia, specialising in fintech, biotechnology, and asset management to drive sustainable growth and long-term value creation. We go beyond traditional investment by delivering advanced lending solutions, pioneering healthcare innovations, and disciplined asset management strategies that create measurable impact. Through the combination of global expertise and deep local insight, we empower businesses to grow, scale, and thrive in competitive markets. Guided by our core values of integrity, innovation, and collaboration, we build enduring partnerships and actively shape the industries of the future. About Numeral XII * * * # Numeral Verticals ## EXPLORE THE WORLD OF INNOVATION, FINANCE AND GROWTH. From concept to creation, every company tells a story of innovation and ambition. Asset Management View Now Financial Technology View Now Bio-Technology View Now Digital Technology View Now Trade & Industry View Now Automotive View Now ## Shared Services Our Shared Services division delivers centralised, high-quality support that strengthens efficiency, governance, and operational excellence across all Numeral entities and investment platforms. By integrating finance, compliance, technology, and administrative capabilities, we ensure seamless execution and consistent standards, enabling our teams and partners to focus on growth, performance, and long-term value creation. Shared Services ## Our Values Integrity, innovation, and collaboration are not just principles – they form the foundation of everything we do. These values underpin our ability to build enduring partnerships and actively shape the industries of the future. ## Our Brands From industry leaders to rising innovators, these are the brands that define our space. At Numeral XII, we align with those who challenge the ordinary, delivering exceptional products, services, and experiences. Additional information: name: RENEW LLC description: An impact-focused investment and management consulting firm country: Algeria website: linkedin: entity_type: investor website_content: []( * []( * []( * []( * []( Us * []( * []( * []( Overview * Training Portal * []( * []( Overview * Investor Portal * []( * []( * Invest * Apply # Early-stage investing in Africa Renew Capital is a pan-Africa investment firm. We back the bold founders using technology to pursue Africa’s greatest opportunities. []( for Investment []( with Us ###### Grow A Company.Build A Nation.Leave A Legacy. OUR PORTFOLIO []( AFRICA IS RISING ## Entrepreneurship is booming on the world’s youngest continent and cell phone access is driving socioeconomic advances and digital transformation. #### 11 of the world's 20 fastest-growing countries #### 2.5B population by the next quarter-century #### 1 in 4 people on the planet will be African by 2050 #### $2.5T in consumer spending by 2030 #### 50% of Africa's population under 25 by 2050 THE OPPORTUNITY ## Africa is in startup mode. Africa is on track for significant growth. Renew Capital believes that by investing in tech and tech-enabled startups and supporting them with management development and market access, we can help companies capture market share, modernize industries, create jobs and realize returns through acquisitions or public offerings. []( Approach .png&w=3840&q=75) KEY RESULTS as of 4/30/2026 50+ Portfolio Companies (Active and Exited) $224k Average Investment Size 200+ Investors 3400 Jobs Created HWM 2025 74% Women's Jobs Supported 13 Countries EARLY-STAGE INVESTING ## Connecting investors to business opportunities in Africa Renew Capital connects its network of individual investors, family offices and institutional investors to early-stage venture capital opportunities through our innovative investment model. []( with Us ECOSYSTEM DEVELOPMENT ## Strengthening Africa’s trade and investment capacity Renew Capital works with founders and ecosystem enablers to strengthen the startup ecosystems in which we invest. This work boosts portfolio companies, the landscape for future investors and the countries of investment. []( We Work Case Study ## Bridging Canada and Africa Through Trade, Innovation, and Capital Renew Capital works at the intersection of private investment and development, mobilizing capital to scale African startups while unlocking new trade and investment opportunities for Canadian firms. Through a blended finance model supported by Global Affairs Canada, we reduce early-stage risk, strengthen Africa’s private sector, and help build long-term, mutually beneficial economic partnerships between Canada and the continent. Name: Stanford Seed – Stanford Institute for Development in Developing Economies
Description: African Investor. Stanford Seed, the Stanford Institute for Innovation in Developing Economies, is working to end the cycle of global poverty by empowering leaders in developing and emerging markets to lead their regions to greater prosperity. We believe that business is one of the most powerful engines of change in developing and emerging economies and that the innovative and entrepreneurial mindset that we foster at Stanford can be leveraged to change lives, organizations, and the world. What does Seed stand for? Seed isn’t an acronym. The name is based on our strongly held belief that it is the growth of the private sector, and especially small and medium enterprises, that will seed prosperity in developing and emerging countries.
Investment focus: private sector, small and medium enterprises, entrepreneurship

Entity type: investor
Name: Sterling Bank
Description: Sterling Bank Plc is a full service national commercial bank licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria. Its shares trade under the Banking sector of the Nigerian Stock Exchange with the symbol STERLNBANK. The bank provides services to individuals, small businesses (SMEs) and large corporations. As of December 2018, the bank’s branch network numbered 179, distributed throughout Nigeria with total assets valued in excess of NGN1 trillion and shareholders’ equity estimated at about NGN97.8 billion). Sterling Bank Plc originally incorporated in 1960 as Nigeria Acceptances Limited (NAL). The bank was licensed as Nigeria?s first merchant bank in 1969. Consequent to the indigenization decree of 1972, the Bank became fully government owned and was managed in partnership with Grindlays Bank Limited, Continental International Finance Company Illinois and American Express Bank Limited between 1974 and 1992. In 1992, the Bank was partly privatized and listed as a public company on the Nigeria Stock Exchange (NSE). Eight years later, in 2000, the Federal Government sold its residual interest in the bank, effectively making it a fully privatized institution. In 2013, the Bank was licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria to provide non-interest banking (Islamic finance) services after meeting the set criteria. The alternative banking products are split into three facets: Transactional, Investment, and Financing. The Bank has set up this arm of business in line with global best practice.
Country: Nigeria
Investment focus: Islamic finance, Transactional, Investment, Financing
